# Asaptic Cross-Standard — full corpus map (llms-full.txt)
# Public-interest, source-linked China-export compliance comparisons. AI-compiled, multi-model cross-verified, NOT human-verified; informational only — verify with the official regulator. Trilingual (en/zh-Hans/zh-Hant). License CC-BY-4.0.
# Updated: 2026-06-15 | 706 comparisons · 23 product categories · 169 markets
# Machine-readable index: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/index.json

## Grid-tied PV inverter
- Grid-tied PV inverter -> Albania (DPS / ERE / OST): For China-made solar inverters exported to Albania, the practical gaps are typically in CE-aligned product conformity evidence, TS EN 62109-based electrical safety evidence, inverter firmware settings for 230/400 V 50 Hz, anti-islanding…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/solar-inverter-china-to-albania
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/solar-inverter-china-to-albania.json

## Battery energy storage (BESS)
- Battery energy storage (BESS) -> Albania (DPS / ERE / OST): For China-made BESS exported to Albania, the practical baseline is CE-aligned product compliance where the equipment is in scope of low-voltage, EMC, radio, and safety-related regimes, with TS EN IEC 62619 and IEC 62933 evidence commonly…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/bess-china-to-albania
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/bess-china-to-albania.json
- Battery energy storage (BESS) -> Algeria (IANOR / CREG / Sonelgaz): For BESS exported from China to Algeria, the main compliance gaps are: IANOR conformity to IEC 62619 for lithium battery safety and IEC 62933 for energy storage system performance, CREG and Sonelgaz/GRTE grid-connection and storage…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/bess-china-to-algeria
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/bess-china-to-algeria.json
- Battery energy storage (BESS) -> Angola (IANORQ / IRSEA / ENDE): Angola does not yet have a confirmed standalone mandatory BESS product safety regulation equivalent to an IEC 62619 mandatory certification regime. The primary BESS deployment context in Angola is solar-plus-storage EPC projects financed…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/bess-china-to-angola
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/bess-china-to-angola.json
- Battery energy storage (BESS) -> Antigua and Barbuda (ABBS / APUA): Antigua and Barbuda does not yet have a confirmed standalone mandatory BESS product safety regulation equivalent to the EU Battery Regulation or a dedicated BESS conformity regime under ABBS. BESS projects operate primarily under…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/bess-china-to-antigua-and-barbuda
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/bess-china-to-antigua-and-barbuda.json
- Battery energy storage (BESS) -> Argentina (IRAM / Sec. Energía): For BESS exported from China to Argentina, there is no single universal Argentina BESS product certificate that automatically replaces project due diligence. The main gaps are IRAM / IEC evidence requested by buyers or engineers, UN 38.3…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/bess-china-to-argentina
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/bess-china-to-argentina.json
- Battery energy storage (BESS) -> Armenia (SARM / PSRC / ENA): Armenia is a member of the Eurasian Economic Union (EAEU), which means EAEU Technical Regulations (TR CU / TR EAEU) and the EAC mark apply to in-scope products — this is a genuine certification pathway for Chinese BESS exporters and is…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/bess-china-to-armenia
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/bess-china-to-armenia.json
- Battery energy storage (BESS) -> Australia (RCM / AS-NZS): RCM / EESS registration, AS/NZS IEC 62619 cell-safety testing at an ILAC-accredited lab, AS/NZS 5139:2019 installation compliance, AS 4777.2:2020 grid protection, and Clean Energy Council approved-product listing are the main gaps beyond…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/bess-china-to-australia
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/bess-china-to-australia.json
- Battery energy storage (BESS) -> Austria (ASI / E-Control / APG): For Austria, the practical entry barriers are mainly EU CE conformity execution (LVD 2014/35/EU, EMC 2014/30/EU, RED 2014/53/EU where applicable, plus Battery Regulation (EU) 2023/1542 lifecycle and due-diligence duties), E-Control / APG…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/bess-china-to-austria
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/bess-china-to-austria.json
- Battery energy storage (BESS) -> Azerbaijan (AZSTAND / Azerishig): For BESS exported from China to Azerbaijan, the common first gap is domestic Azerbaijan conformity through AZSTAND / AZS national standards and mandatory conformity documentation where in scope, not EAEU EAC certification. Follow-on gaps…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/bess-china-to-azerbaijan
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/bess-china-to-azerbaijan.json
- Battery energy storage (BESS) -> Bahamas (BBSQ / URCA / BPL): The Bahamas presents a fundamentally different technical baseline from China for BESS exports. The most critical gap is the grid frequency and voltage mismatch: the Bahamas operates on a 60 Hz / 120–240 V (single-phase) / 208–240 V…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/bess-china-to-bahamas
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/bess-china-to-bahamas.json
- Battery energy storage (BESS) -> Bahrain (BSMD / EWA): For BESS exported from China to Bahrain, public sources do not show one universal mandatory whole-product BESS certificate at the import level. The practical compliance route involves BSMD standards screening, IEC 62619:2022 safety…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/bess-china-to-bahrain
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/bess-china-to-bahrain.json
- Battery energy storage (BESS) -> Bangladesh (BSTI / SREDA): For BESS exported from China to Bangladesh, public official sources do not establish a blanket whole-product mandatory BSTI approval for lithium BESS. The practical gap is route confirmation: screen the exact shipment and components for…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/bess-china-to-bangladesh
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/bess-china-to-bangladesh.json
- Battery energy storage (BESS) -> Barbados (BNSI / FTC / BL&P): Barbados has a nascent standalone BESS regulatory layer, so projects are cleared through a functional chain: Barbados National Standards Institution (BNSI) conformity assessment with IEC references, BL&P interconnection approval, and FTC…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/bess-china-to-barbados
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/bess-china-to-barbados.json

## EV charger
- EV charger -> Albania (DPS / ERE / OST): For China-made EV chargers exported to Albania, the practical gaps are CE-aligned product and EMC readiness, TS EN IEC 61851 electrical-safety evidence, Type 2 / CCS2 connector and locking compatibility, OCPP backend acceptance, ERE /…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/ev-charger-china-to-albania
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/ev-charger-china-to-albania.json
- EV charger -> Algeria (IANOR / CREG / Sonelgaz): For China-made EV chargers entering Algeria, the practical compliance gaps are IEC-route evidence and grid acceptance rather than China domestic approvals. Algeria's standards system is anchored by IANOR, while electricity regulation and…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/ev-charger-china-to-algeria
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/ev-charger-china-to-algeria.json
- EV charger -> Angola (IANORQ / IRSEA / ENDE): For China-made EV chargers entering Angola, the practical gaps are not a mature, published EVSE-specific rulebook but the market-access and project gates around IANORQ conformity/import review, IEC-based safety and EMC evidence, IRSEA…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/ev-charger-china-to-angola
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/ev-charger-china-to-angola.json
- EV charger -> Antigua and Barbuda (ABBS / APUA): For China-made EV chargers entering Antigua and Barbuda, the practical gaps are IEC-based safety and EMC evidence, connector redesign from GB/T 20234 to IEC 62196 Type 2 / CCS2 for the UK/IEC-legacy Caribbean environment, APUA grid and…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/ev-charger-china-to-antigua-and-barbuda
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/ev-charger-china-to-antigua-and-barbuda.json

  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/ev-charger-china-to-argentina
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/ev-charger-china-to-argentina.json
- EV charger -> Armenia (SARM / PSRC / ENA): For China-made EV chargers entering Armenia, the confirmed practical gaps are EAEU Technical Regulation and EAC conformity for in-scope products, IEC-family EVSE safety evidence, connector conversion from GB/T 20234 to IEC 62196 Type 2…

- EV charger -> Australia (RCM / AS-NZS): EESS Level 3 registration, RCM mark, Type 2 / CCS2 connector hardware redesign (GB/T 20234 connectors are incompatible), and fresh AS/NZS 61851.1 and ACMA EMC testing are the top Australian gaps for Chinese EV chargers.
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/ev-charger-china-to-australia
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/ev-charger-china-to-australia.json

- EV charger -> Morocco (IMANOR / ONEE): For EV chargers exported from China to Morocco, the critical gap is the connector standard: Morocco's IMANOR pathway aligns with IEC 62196-2 (Type 2, Mennekes) for AC charging and IEC 62196-3 (CCS Combo 2) for DC fast charging, whereas…

  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/ev-charger-china-to-us
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/ev-charger-china-to-us.json
- EV charger -> Uzbekistan (O'zstandart / Uzenergo): For EV chargers exported from China to Uzbekistan, China GB/T 18487 and GB/T 20234 evidence is useful technical documentation but does not by itself close Uzbekistan market-access gaps. Uzbekistan is not an EAEU member — the domestic…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/ev-charger-china-to-uzbekistan
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/ev-charger-china-to-uzbekistan.json
- EV charger -> Vanuatu (URA / UNELCO / VUI): For China-made EV chargers entering Vanuatu, the practical gaps are IEC-aligned product evidence, connector conversion from GB/T 20234 to IEC 62196 Type 2 for AC and CCS2 for DC, URA-regulated utility or concessionaire project acceptance…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/ev-charger-china-to-vanuatu
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/ev-charger-china-to-vanuatu.json
- EV charger -> Vietnam (MOIT / TCVN / EVN): For China-made EV chargers entering Vietnam, the main practical gaps are TCVN / IEC 61851 evidence, GB/T 20234-to-Type 2 or CCS2 connector redesign where the project specifies IEC connectors, MOIT and EVN charging-station and grid…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/ev-charger-china-to-vietnam
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/ev-charger-china-to-vietnam.json
- EV charger -> Zambia (ZABS / ERB / ZESCO): For China-made EV chargers entering Zambia, the confirmed practical gaps are: connector hardware redesign from GB/T 20234 to IEC 62196 Type 2 (AC) / CCS2 (DC), since Zambia's IEC-aligned direction is physically incompatible with GB/T…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/ev-charger-china-to-zambia
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/ev-charger-china-to-zambia.json
- EV charger -> Zimbabwe (SAZ / ZERA / ZESA): For China-made EV chargers entering Zimbabwe, the practical gaps are IEC-based safety, EMC, and connector evidence through SAZ-aligned conformity expectations; hardware conversion from GB/T 20234 to IEC 62196 Type 2 for AC and CCS2 for…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/ev-charger-china-to-zimbabwe
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/ev-charger-china-to-zimbabwe.json

## Home/clinical medical device
- Home/clinical medical device -> Australia (TGA / ARTG): Chinese manufacturers entering Australia must appoint an Australian Sponsor, obtain ARTG inclusion under TGA classification, hold an ISO 13485 certificate from a TGA-recognised CAB, demonstrate AS/NZS IEC 60601-1 electrical safety…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/medical-device-china-to-australia
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/medical-device-china-to-australia.json
- Home/clinical medical device -> Brazil (ANVISA): Brazil requires ANVISA registration or notification (RDC 751/2022 Classes I–IV), Brazilian GMP certification (B-GMP, mandatory for Class III/IV), INMETRO electromedical certification (ABNT NBR IEC 60601-1), a Brazil-incorporated…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/medical-device-china-to-brazil
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/medical-device-china-to-brazil.json
- Home/clinical medical device -> Canada (Health Canada / MDSAP): MDSAP-certified ISO 13485 QMS (mandatory for Class II–IV manufacturers since 2019 via MDL), a Canadian MDL, an MDEL for the importer (requiring documented procedures per s.45 — not an ISO 13485 certificate), bilingual English-and-French…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/medical-device-china-to-canada
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/medical-device-china-to-canada.json
- Home/clinical medical device -> European Union (MDR): EU MDR 2017/745 conformity, usually through a Notified Body, an EU Authorised Representative, EUDAMED and UDI obligations, and ISO 13485 QMS evidence are the main gaps beyond NMPA; IEC 60601 safety largely transfers from GB 9706.
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/medical-device-china-to-eu
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/medical-device-china-to-eu.json
- Home/clinical medical device -> India (CDSCO): Appointing an Indian Authorized Agent and obtaining a CDSCO Import Licence (Form MD-15 via MD-14 application on SUGAM) are the primary structural gaps versus NMPA; IS/IEC 60601-1 safety largely transfers from GB 9706.1-2020 given their…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/medical-device-china-to-india
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/medical-device-china-to-india.json
- Home/clinical medical device -> Japan (PMDA / PMD Act): Japan's PMD Act requires a licensed Japanese Marketing Authorization Holder (MAH) — a Chinese manufacturer cannot hold the authorization directly — plus Foreign Manufacturer Registration, MHLW Ordinance 169 QMS compliance (with possible…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/medical-device-china-to-japan
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/medical-device-china-to-japan.json
- Home/clinical medical device -> Mexico (COFEPRIS): Mexico generally requires COFEPRIS sanitary registration before sale or import of medical devices, local Class I/II/III classification, applicable NOM compliance including NOM-241-SSA1 GMP and Spanish labelling under NOM-137-SSA1, plus a…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/medical-device-china-to-mexico
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/medical-device-china-to-mexico.json
- Home/clinical medical device -> Saudi Arabia (SFDA): SFDA Medical Device Marketing Authorization (MDMA), a Saudi-based Authorized Representative, ISO 13485 QMS certification by an SFDA-recognized body, and Arabic-language labelling are the primary new requirements beyond NMPA; devices…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/medical-device-china-to-saudi
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/medical-device-china-to-saudi.json
- Home/clinical medical device -> United Kingdom (UKCA / MHRA): UKCA marking (or CE under transitional acceptance expiring by class by 2030), a mandatory UK Responsible Person registered on MHRA DORS, and ISO 13485 QMS assessed by a UK Approved Body are the main gaps beyond NMPA; BS EN 60601-1…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/medical-device-china-to-uk
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/medical-device-china-to-uk.json
- Home/clinical medical device -> United States (FDA): FDA 510(k) clearance, establishment registration and device listing, a US Agent, and 21 CFR 820 QMS are the main gaps beyond NMPA; IEC 60601 safety largely transfers from GB 9706.
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/medical-device-china-to-us
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/medical-device-china-to-us.json

## Industrial electric motor
- Industrial electric motor -> Australia (GEMS / RCM): GEMS Registry pre-supply registration (unique to Australia, no CN equivalent) and the RCM mark (combining electrical safety and EMC) are the critical market-access gaps; IE3 MEPS floors are nominally aligned but test method and numeric…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/industrial-motor-china-to-australia
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/industrial-motor-china-to-australia.json
- Industrial electric motor -> Brazil (INMETRO): INMETRO compulsory certification and Lei 10.295/2001 premium efficiency (IR3) are the primary gaps; Chinese motors must obtain an INMETRO conformity mark and meet ABNT NBR 17094 safety requirements before Brazilian market entry.
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/industrial-motor-china-to-brazil
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/industrial-motor-china-to-brazil.json
- Industrial electric motor -> European Union (CE): EU Ecodesign Regulation 2019/1781 minimum efficiency (IE3/IE4) and CE conformity for LVD/EMC are the gaps; many Chinese motors fall below the EU MEPS class.
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/industrial-motor-china-to-eu
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/industrial-motor-china-to-eu.json
- Industrial electric motor -> India (BIS / BEE): IS 12615:2018 is the applicable Indian efficiency standard and BIS ISI mark under FMCS is the certification route for foreign manufacturers IF required. Whether BIS ISI mark certification is currently mandatory under a valid in-force QCO…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/industrial-motor-china-to-india
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/industrial-motor-china-to-india.json
- Industrial electric motor -> Japan (Top Runner): Japan's Top Runner Program mandates IE3-equivalent minimum efficiency for 3-phase induction motors (0.75–375 kW) under the Act on Rationalizing Energy Use; Chinese motors meeting GB 18613-2020 Grade 3 (IE3) are technically close but…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/industrial-motor-china-to-japan
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/industrial-motor-china-to-japan.json
- Industrial electric motor -> Mexico (NOM / CONUEE): Mexico is not a CCC carry-over market: three-phase induction motors must be screened against NOM-016-ENER-2016 under CONUEE, mapped to applicable electrical safety NOMs such as NOM-001-SEDE and NOM-003-SCFI, and certified through an…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/industrial-motor-china-to-mexico
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/industrial-motor-china-to-mexico.json
- Industrial electric motor -> Saudi Arabia (SASO / SEEC): Saudi Arabia requires IE3 minimum efficiency (SASO/SEEC regulation referencing IEC 60034-30-1) and SABER PCoC+SCoC market-access conformity certificates; most Chinese motors meet the IE3 efficiency floor but must obtain SABER…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/industrial-motor-china-to-saudi
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/industrial-motor-china-to-saudi.json
- Industrial electric motor -> United Kingdom (UKCA): UK post-Brexit regulations mirror the EU framework: IE3/IE4 MEPS (retained Ecodesign via SI 2021/745), Electrical Equipment Safety Regulations 2016, and UK EMC Regulations 2016 all apply — the key additional gaps versus EU are replacing…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/industrial-motor-china-to-uk
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/industrial-motor-china-to-uk.json
- Industrial electric motor -> United States (DOE / NEMA): US DOE energy conservation standards, NEMA Premium or IE3-equivalent efficiency expectations, NEMA MG-1 design evidence, UL listing, and IEEE 112 Method B testing are the main gaps; GB/T 1032 testing alone is not the US basis.
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/industrial-motor-china-to-us
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/industrial-motor-china-to-us.json

## Sodium-ion battery storage (Na-ion)
- Sodium-ion battery storage (Na-ion) -> European Union (CE): Na-ion faces the same EU Battery Regulation 2023/1542 obligations as Li-ion, including carbon footprint, due diligence, and battery passport requirements, plus CE and EN IEC 62619 expectations; one upside is easier UN 38.3 transport…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/na-ion-storage-china-to-eu
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/na-ion-storage-china-to-eu.json
- Sodium-ion battery storage (Na-ion) -> United Kingdom (UKCA / CE for Great Britain): Na-ion BESS entering Great Britain must be assessed under the applicable UK product-safety SIs for UKCA or currently recognised CE marking, with BS EN IEC 62619 used only as a voluntary presumption-of-conformity route where accepted;…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/na-ion-storage-china-to-uk
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/na-ion-storage-china-to-uk.json
- Sodium-ion battery storage (Na-ion) -> United States (UL/NFPA): UL 9540 system listing, UL 1973 cell and battery evidence, and NFPA 855 installation requirements are the main gaps; Na-ion sits in the same UL and NFPA framework as Li-ion.
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/na-ion-storage-china-to-us
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/na-ion-storage-china-to-us.json

## Perovskite / tandem PV module
- Perovskite / tandem PV module -> European Union (CE): IEC 61215 and IEC 61730 module qualification, REACH SVHC notification for lead compounds (Article 33), and WEEE producer registration are the key gaps beyond conventional silicon PV modules. Note: PV panels are explicitly excluded from…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/perovskite-pv-china-to-eu
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/perovskite-pv-china-to-eu.json
- Perovskite / tandem PV module -> United States (UL): UL 61730 listing, fire classification, and lead-leaching scrutiny such as EPA TCLP are the major gaps for perovskite modules.
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/perovskite-pv-china-to-us
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/perovskite-pv-china-to-us.json

## Water electrolyzer (green hydrogen, AEM/PEM)
- Water electrolyzer (green hydrogen, AEM/PEM) -> European Union (CE): Beyond CE, the Pressure Equipment Directive (PED 2014/68/EU), ATEX requirements for hydrogen explosive atmospheres, and ISO 22734 are the major gaps.
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/electrolyzer-china-to-eu
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/electrolyzer-china-to-eu.json
- Water electrolyzer (green hydrogen, AEM/PEM) -> United States (ASME/NFPA): ASME pressure stamp, NFPA 2 Hydrogen Technologies Code, and NRTL listing are the main gaps.
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/electrolyzer-china-to-us
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/electrolyzer-china-to-us.json

## Data-center liquid cooling (cold plate / CDU)
- Data-center liquid cooling (cold plate / CDU) -> European Union (CE): Beyond CE (LVD/Machinery/EMC), the gaps are pressurised coolant-loop safety, RoHS/REACH plus F-gas/PFAS coolant rules, and EU server/data-centre Ecodesign (Reg 2019/424) plus Energy Efficiency Directive reporting.
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/datacenter-cooling-china-to-eu
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/datacenter-cooling-china-to-eu.json
- Data-center liquid cooling (cold plate / CDU) -> United States (UL/NFPA): UL/NRTL listing of the cooling unit, NFPA fire/liquid handling, ASHRAE practice, and EPA PFAS coolant scrutiny are the gaps vs Chinese GB.
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/datacenter-cooling-china-to-us
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/datacenter-cooling-china-to-us.json

## GaN fast charger / power adapter

  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/gan-charger-china-to-eu
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/gan-charger-china-to-eu.json
- GaN fast charger / power adapter -> United Kingdom / Great Britain (UKCA and accepted CE): For Great Britain, the core gaps are UKCA or accepted CE marking under the Electrical Equipment (Safety) Regulations 2016 and EMC Regulations 2016, UK RoHS compliance, and a compliant BS 1363 plug under the Plugs and Sockets etc.…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/gan-charger-china-to-uk
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/gan-charger-china-to-uk.json
- GaN fast charger / power adapter -> United States (UL/DoE): UL 62368-1 NRTL listing plus FCC Part 15 plus DoE Level VI external-power-supply efficiency plus California CEC registration are the gaps vs Chinese GB.
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/gan-charger-china-to-us

  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/heat-pump-china-to-switzerland.json
- Air-source heat pump -> United Kingdom (UKCA / MCS): UKCA marking (under retained Ecodesign, EMC, and product safety SIs), F-gas quota compliance under SI 2022/1013, and pressure equipment conformity under SI 2016/1105 are the mandatory gates for air-source heat pumps entering the UK…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/heat-pump-china-to-uk
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/heat-pump-china-to-uk.json
- Air-source heat pump -> United States (DOE / AHRI / UL): DOE minimum efficiency standards (HSPF2/SEER2), UL 60335-2-40 NRTL safety listing, and EPA refrigerant SNAP approval are the three mandatory gates for air-source heat pumps entering the US market from China.
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/heat-pump-china-to-us
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/heat-pump-china-to-us.json

## LED luminaire
- LED luminaire -> Australia (RCM / EESS / ACMA / GEMS): A Chinese LED luminaire manufacturer entering Australia must have an Australian responsible supplier/importer, meet electrical safety obligations under state and territory law and the EESS framework, apply the RCM where required, build a…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/led-luminaire-china-to-australia
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/led-luminaire-china-to-australia.json
- LED luminaire -> Brazil (INMETRO): A Chinese LED luminaire manufacturer entering the Brazil market must obtain INMETRO compulsory certification through an accredited certification body (OCP) under Portaria INMETRO 389/2021, with testing to ABNT NBR IEC 60598-1…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/led-luminaire-china-to-brazil
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/led-luminaire-china-to-brazil.json
- LED luminaire -> Canada (SCC / ISED / NRCan): A Chinese LED luminaire manufacturer entering Canada should plan for Canadian electrical safety approval through an SCC-accredited certification body using the CSA C22.2 No. 250 series or the applicable Canadian national standard; ISED…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/led-luminaire-china-to-canada
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/led-luminaire-china-to-canada.json

  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/led-luminaire-china-to-chile
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/led-luminaire-china-to-chile.json
- LED luminaire -> Egypt (EOS / GOEIC): A Chinese LED luminaire manufacturer exporting to Egypt must ensure the product conforms to Egyptian EOS (ES) standards that adopt IEC 60598 (luminaire safety), IEC 62560 (self-ballasted LED lamp safety), and IEC 62471 (photobiological…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/led-luminaire-china-to-egypt
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/led-luminaire-china-to-egypt.json
- LED luminaire -> European Union (CE / ErP): A Chinese LED luminaire manufacturer entering the EU market must obtain CE marking under LVD (EN 60598-1), EMC (EN 55015 / EN 61547), and RoHS directives; comply with Ecodesign minimum efficacy, CRI, lifetime, and power factor…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/led-luminaire-china-to-eu
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/led-luminaire-china-to-eu.json
- LED luminaire -> India (BIS CRS / BEE): A Chinese LED luminaire or LED lamp manufacturer entering India must first confirm whether the exact product is notified under the Electronics and IT Goods (Requirements for Compulsory Registration) framework administered through BIS…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/led-luminaire-china-to-india
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/led-luminaire-china-to-india.json
- LED luminaire -> Indonesia (SNI / Kemenperin): A Chinese LED luminaire manufacturer exporting to Indonesia must obtain mandatory SNI certification (SPPT-SNI plus the right to apply the SNI mark) issued by an accredited LSPro through type testing and an audit of the factory quality…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/led-luminaire-china-to-indonesia
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/led-luminaire-china-to-indonesia.json
- LED luminaire -> Japan (PSE/JET): A Chinese LED luminaire manufacturer entering the Japan market must comply with the DENAN (Electrical Appliances and Materials Safety Act): determine whether the product is a Specified product (requiring the diamond ◇ PSE mark via…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/led-luminaire-china-to-japan

- LED luminaire -> Kenya (KEBS): A Chinese LED luminaire manufacturer exporting to Kenya must route the consignment through the KEBS Pre-Export Verification of Conformity (PVoC) programme to obtain a Certificate of Conformity (CoC) before shipment; comply with the…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/led-luminaire-china-to-kenya
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/led-luminaire-china-to-kenya.json
- LED luminaire -> Malaysia (ST / SIRIM): Register the Malaysian importer route, then verify ST CoA and SIRIM alignment against Chinese test data, and finally check any wireless or telecom add-ons separately.
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/led-luminaire-china-to-malaysia
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/led-luminaire-china-to-malaysia.json
- LED luminaire -> Mexico (NOM / NYCE / ANCE): A Chinese LED luminaire manufacturer entering Mexico should plan for Mexican NOM conformity before shipment: screen the luminaire and LED driver against NOM-058-SCFI luminaire safety and NOM-003-SCFI electrical-product safety scopes;…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/led-luminaire-china-to-mexico

  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/led-luminaire-china-to-peru.json

  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/led-luminaire-china-to-philippines
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/led-luminaire-china-to-philippines.json
- LED luminaire -> Saudi Arabia (SASO/SABER): A Chinese LED luminaire manufacturer entering the Saudi market must register the product on the SABER platform and obtain a Product Certificate of Conformity (PCoC) from a SASO-approved Conformity Assessment Body, then a Shipment…

- LED luminaire -> United Arab Emirates (MoIAT/ESMA — ECAS): A Chinese LED luminaire manufacturer entering the UAE market must register the product under the Emirates Conformity Assessment Scheme (ECAS) administered by MoIAT (incorporating the former ESMA) and obtain a Certificate of Conformity…

- LED luminaire -> United Kingdom (UKCA): LED luminaires exported from China to the UK require UKCA marking (or CE under current transitional easement), compliance with BS EN 60598, ecodesign efficacy thresholds under SI 2021/1095, and a UK Responsible Person — the biggest gap…

- LED luminaire -> United States (UL / DLC / FCC): NRTL certification (UL 1598 + UL 8750) and FCC Part 15B SDoC are the critical US gaps for Chinese LED luminaires. Note: there is no single federal consumer safety mark in the US — NRTL listing is effectively required via the National…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/led-luminaire-china-to-us
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/led-luminaire-china-to-us.json
- LED luminaire -> Vietnam (TCVN / QCVN): A Chinese LED luminaire manufacturer exporting to Vietnam must obtain certification of conformity to QCVN 19:2019/BKHCN (the mandatory national technical regulation for lighting equipment EMC and safety) and affix the CR conformity mark…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/led-luminaire-china-to-vietnam

## Electric bicycle (e-bike)
- Electric bicycle (e-bike) -> Australia: For Australia, the critical China-to-AU gap is classification: a road-legal e-bike is generally either a pedalec complying with EN 15194 and ≤250W continuous rated power, or a power-assisted pedal cycle with auxiliary motor output ≤200W…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/e-bike-china-to-australia
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/e-bike-china-to-australia.json
- Electric bicycle (e-bike) -> Canada (federal MVSA/MVSR, provincial road rules, CSA/SCC electrical certification): Canada is not a CE-style self-declaration market: e-bikes must fit federal import classification and provincial road-use limits, commonly the former power-assisted bicycle benchmark of ≤500W and motor assistance not above 32km/h, while…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/e-bike-china-to-canada
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/e-bike-china-to-canada.json
- Electric bicycle (e-bike) -> European Union (CE / EN 15194): Pedelec e-bikes ≤250W/25km/h need CE marking under EN 15194:2017 (Machinery Directive); Chinese motors rated 251–400W trigger full EU type-approval under Regulation (EU) 168/2013 (L1e-A) — the critical export-blocking gap.
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/e-bike-china-to-eu
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/e-bike-china-to-eu.json
- Electric bicycle (e-bike) -> United Kingdom (UKCA / EAPC): Motor power (≤250 W UK vs ≤400 W CN), UKCA marking under BS EN 15194:2017, BS EN 50604-1 battery re-testing, and appointment of a UK Responsible Person are the four major gaps for Chinese e-bikes entering the UK market.
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/e-bike-china-to-uk
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/e-bike-china-to-uk.json
- Electric bicycle (e-bike) -> United States (CPSC / UL 2849): UL 2849 electrical-system and UL 2271 battery certification (city-mandatory in NYC under Local Law 39), CPSC 16 CFR 1512 bicycle safety, and federal 15 USC 2085 low-speed e-bike classification are the primary US compliance gaps for…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/e-bike-china-to-us
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/e-bike-china-to-us.json

## Toys & children’s products
- Toys & children’s products -> Australia (ACCC mandatory safety standards / AS/NZS ISO 8124): Australia does not use a separate toy conformity mark comparable to the European marking route. The main China-to-Australia gaps are compliance with ACCC mandatory safety standards under Australian Consumer Law, small-parts and…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/toys-china-to-australia
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/toys-china-to-australia.json
- Toys & children’s products -> Canada (CCPSA / Toys Regulations): Canada has no CE-style single toy mark. The main gaps versus China GB 6675 / CCC are CCPSA importer duties, Toys Regulations mechanical and flammability rules, separate Canadian chemical limits for surface coatings and phthalates, and…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/toys-china-to-canada
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/toys-china-to-canada.json
- Toys & children’s products -> European Union (CE / Toy Safety Directive): EN 71-3 chemical migration (expanded to 19 elements in 2019), REACH substance restrictions, EU Authorised Representative appointment, and multi-language labelling are the major EU gaps compared with Chinese GB 6675 / CCC toy compliance.
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/toys-china-to-eu
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/toys-china-to-eu.json
- Toys & children’s products -> United Kingdom (UKCA / Toys Safety Regs): Chinese toy exporters targeting the UK Great Britain market must comply with the Toys (Safety) Regulations 2011 (SI 2011/1881), which requires testing to UK designated standards (BS EN 71-1, BS EN 71-2, BS EN 71-3, BS EN IEC 62115 for…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/toys-china-to-uk
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/toys-china-to-uk.json
- Toys & children’s products -> United States (CPSC / ASTM F963): The five major compliance gaps for Chinese toys entering the US market are: mandatory ASTM F963 third-party testing (Chinese GB 6675/CCC does not substitute), CPSIA substrate lead limit of 100 ppm (total content, not covered by Chinese…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/toys-china-to-us
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/toys-china-to-us.json

## Cosmetics & personal care
- Cosmetics & personal care -> Australia: Chinese cosmetics exporters to Australia must confirm whether each ingredient is an industrial chemical, ensure the Australian importer is registered with AICIS where required, categorise every chemical introduction before import,…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/cosmetics-china-to-australia
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/cosmetics-china-to-australia.json
- Cosmetics & personal care -> Canada (Food and Drugs Act / Cosmetic Regulations): Chinese cosmetics exporters can generally sell cosmetics in Canada without pre-market approval, but the product must be safe, comply with the Food and Drugs Act and Cosmetic Regulations, meet Hotlist ingredient restrictions, be…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/cosmetics-china-to-canada
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/cosmetics-china-to-canada.json
- Cosmetics & personal care -> European Union (Reg 1223/2009): Chinese cosmetics exporters must appoint an EU-established Responsible Person, complete CPNP pre-market notification, commission a CPSR from a qualified EU safety assessor, verify all ingredients against EU Annex II–VI substance lists,…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/cosmetics-china-to-eu
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/cosmetics-china-to-eu.json
- Cosmetics & personal care -> United Kingdom (UK Cosmetics Regulation): Appointing a UK Responsible Person and completing SCPN notification, obtaining a CPSR from a qualified assessor, screening formulations against current UK substance schedules (which are diverging from EU post-Brexit), ensuring GMP per…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/cosmetics-china-to-uk
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/cosmetics-china-to-uk.json
- Cosmetics & personal care -> United States (FDA / MoCRA): MoCRA facility registration, product listing, safety substantiation, and adverse event reporting are the core new US obligations; sunscreen reformulation (OTC drug, not cosmetic) and color additive pre-approval under 21 CFR 73/74 are the…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/cosmetics-china-to-us
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/cosmetics-china-to-us.json

## Food-contact materials
- Food-contact materials -> Australia: Australia does not operate an EU-style food-contact material authorisation or positive-list system for all packaging materials. Chinese exporters should instead demonstrate that the material will not make food unsafe or unsuitable under…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/food-contact-china-to-australia
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/food-contact-china-to-australia.json
- Food-contact materials -> Canada (Food and Drug Regulations Division 23): Chinese food-contact material exporters targeting Canada must satisfy the Food and Drugs Act and Food and Drug Regulations Division 23 safety rule that packaging must not yield harmful substances to food. Canada generally has no EU-style…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/food-contact-china-to-canada
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/food-contact-china-to-canada.json
- Food-contact materials -> European Union (Reg 1935/2004): Chinese food-contact material exporters targeting the EU must satisfy Reg 1935/2004 safety and inertness requirements, check every intentionally added substance against the EU Union list (Reg 10/2011 for plastics), re-run migration tests…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/food-contact-china-to-eu
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/food-contact-china-to-eu.json
- Food-contact materials -> United Kingdom (retained 1935/2004 / FSA): UK-retained Regulation 1935/2004, UK-assimilated Plastics Regulation 10/2011 (substance authorisation + migration limits + Annex III testing), GMP Regulation 2023/2006, and a written English-language Declaration of Compliance are the…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/food-contact-china-to-uk
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/food-contact-china-to-uk.json
- Food-contact materials -> United States (FDA 21 CFR 174-179): The US FDA regulates food-contact materials through a substance-by-substance approval system under 21 CFR 174-179 — critically, FDA issues no premarket certificate or compliance mark, so manufacturers must self-certify compliance before…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/food-contact-china-to-us
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/food-contact-china-to-us.json

## Lithium battery / power bank
- Lithium battery / power bank -> Australia: Chinese lithium battery and power bank exporters must check whether the product contains button/coin cells covered by ACCC mandatory standards, appoint or work through an Australian Responsible Supplier for in-scope electrical equipment,…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/lithium-battery-china-to-australia

- Lithium battery / power bank -> Brazil (INMETRO): Chinese lithium battery and power bank exporters must obtain INMETRO compulsory certification under Portaria INMETRO 489/2018 (testing to ABNT NBR IEC 62133) issued by an accredited OCP, register the product with INMETRO through an…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/lithium-battery-china-to-brazil
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/lithium-battery-china-to-brazil.json
- Lithium battery / power bank -> Canada: Chinese lithium battery and power bank exporters to Canada should not rely on CCC, GB 31241, or a CE-style single mark. Canada typically requires TDG/UN 38.3 transport documentation, CCPSA consumer product safety controls, bilingual…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/lithium-battery-china-to-canada
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/lithium-battery-china-to-canada.json
- Lithium battery / power bank -> Chile (SEC): Chinese lithium battery and power bank exporters to Chile generally need SEC scope clearance and an importer-led evidence chain including in-scope SEC labeling pathway, INN NCh/IEC 62133 safety alignment, SUBTEL radio approvals for…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/lithium-battery-china-to-chile

- Lithium battery / power bank -> Egypt (EOS / GOEIC): Chinese lithium battery and power bank exporters to Egypt must meet EOS (ES) safety standards that adopt IEC 62133, register the importer and factory with GOEIC under Decree 43/2016, obtain NTRA approval for any wireless feature, and…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/lithium-battery-china-to-egypt

- Lithium battery / power bank -> European Union (Battery Reg 2023/1542): Chinese lithium battery and power bank exporters must obtain EN IEC 62133-2 testing, CE marking under LVD and Battery Regulation 2023/1542, an EU Authorised Representative, and EPR registration per Member State — none of which are…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/lithium-battery-china-to-eu
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/lithium-battery-china-to-eu.json
- Lithium battery / power bank -> India (BIS CRS / DGCA transport): Chinese lithium battery and power bank exporters to India should treat BIS CRS registration as a separate Indian market-access gate: sealed secondary cells/batteries are covered by IS 16046 / IS 16047 technical benchmarks, power banks…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/lithium-battery-china-to-india
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/lithium-battery-china-to-india.json
- Lithium battery / power bank -> Indonesia (SNI / Kemenperin): For lithium cells, battery packs, and power banks exported from China to Indonesia, the key requirement is mandatory in-country compliance. Products in mandatory-SNI scope need BSN-recognized conformity assessment and SPPT-SNI marking,…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/lithium-battery-china-to-indonesia
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/lithium-battery-china-to-indonesia.json

  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/lithium-battery-china-to-japan
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/lithium-battery-china-to-japan.json
- Lithium battery / power bank -> Kenya (KEBS): Chinese lithium battery and power bank exporters to Kenya must pass KEBS Pre-Export Verification of Conformity (PVoC) to obtain a Certificate of Conformity, apply the Import Standardization Mark (ISM) on a KS IEC 62133 basis, obtain…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/lithium-battery-china-to-kenya

- Lithium battery / power bank -> Malaysia (ST / SIRIM): Chinese lithium battery and power bank exporters targeting Malaysia must obtain MS IEC 62133 cell safety testing, an Energy Commission (Suruhanjaya Tenaga / ST) Certificate of Approval where the product is in scope, SIRIM QAS…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/lithium-battery-china-to-malaysia

- Lithium battery / power bank -> Mexico (NOM electrical safety and Spanish labelling): Chinese lithium battery and power bank exporters to Mexico should treat NOM compliance as a separate market-access project: in-scope power banks and electronic products may require NOM-001-SCFI safety certification through NYCE or…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/lithium-battery-china-to-mexico

- Lithium battery / power bank -> Morocco (IMANOR): Chinese lithium battery and power bank exporters to Morocco must meet IMANOR NM standards (adopting IEC 62133) for cell safety, obtain Ministry of Industry import conformity (CMIM-type attestation of conformity), secure ANRT approval for…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/lithium-battery-china-to-morocco
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/lithium-battery-china-to-morocco.json
- Lithium battery / power bank -> Nigeria (SON / SONCAP): Chinese lithium battery and power bank exporters to Nigeria must obtain a SONCAP Product Certificate plus a per-shipment SONCAP Certificate from the Standards Organisation of Nigeria on the NIS IEC 62133 safety basis, NCC type approval…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/lithium-battery-china-to-nigeria
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/lithium-battery-china-to-nigeria.json
- Lithium battery / power bank -> Peru (INACAL): Chinese lithium battery and power bank exporters to Peru should meet IEC 62133-based safety (adopted as NTP by INACAL), comply with any applicable Reglamento Tecnico requiring certification, obtain MTC equipment homologation for wireless…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/lithium-battery-china-to-peru
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/lithium-battery-china-to-peru.json
- Lithium battery / power bank -> Philippines (BPS / DTI): For lithium batteries and power banks from China to the Philippines, the practical gate is Philippine in-country compliance. BPS PS or ICC evidence and any required import-side marking are destination-specific. Wireless-enabled products…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/lithium-battery-china-to-philippines
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/lithium-battery-china-to-philippines.json
- Lithium battery / power bank -> Saudi Arabia (SASO/SABER): Chinese lithium battery and power bank exporters must register the product on the Saudi SABER platform to obtain a Product Certificate of Conformity (PCoC) and a per-shipment Shipment Certificate of Conformity (SCoC), demonstrate…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/lithium-battery-china-to-saudi
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/lithium-battery-china-to-saudi.json

  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/lithium-battery-china-to-singapore
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/lithium-battery-china-to-singapore.json
- Lithium battery / power bank -> South Africa (NRCS): Chinese lithium battery and power bank exporters selling into South Africa must work through an in-country importer, obtain an NRCS Letter of Authority where the product falls under a Compulsory Specification (VC), build safety evidence…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/lithium-battery-china-to-south-africa
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/lithium-battery-china-to-south-africa.json
- Lithium battery / power bank -> South Korea (KC / KATS): Chinese lithium battery and power bank exporters must obtain KC Safety Certification (안전인증) under the Electrical Appliances and Consumer Products Safety Control Act with KC 62133 testing by a KATS-designated lab (KTC / KTL / KTR), KC-EMC…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/lithium-battery-china-to-south-korea
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/lithium-battery-china-to-south-korea.json
- Lithium battery / power bank -> Thailand (TISI): Major gaps are Thailand mandatory TIS conformity pathways for controlled battery products, mandatory or function-linked NBTC requirements for radio features, and country-specific importer/licence and port-clearance workflow. UN 38.3…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/lithium-battery-china-to-thailand
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/lithium-battery-china-to-thailand.json
- Lithium battery / power bank -> Turkey (TSE / CE): Chinese lithium battery and power bank exporters must obtain TS EN IEC 62133-2 testing, CE marking under the EU–Turkey Customs Union (LVD/EMC/RED-aligned Turkish technical regulations), and appoint a Turkey-established importer or…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/lithium-battery-china-to-turkey
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/lithium-battery-china-to-turkey.json
- Lithium battery / power bank -> United Arab Emirates (MoIAT/ESMA — ECAS): For China-made lithium batteries and power banks entering the UAE, the confirmed practical gaps are MoIAT/ESMA ECAS scope mapping, in-country importer or registrant involvement, UAE.S and GSO-referenced safety expectations via IEC 62133…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/lithium-battery-china-to-uae
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/lithium-battery-china-to-uae.json
- Lithium battery / power bank -> United Kingdom (UKCA): BS EN IEC 62133-2 cell/pack safety testing, UN 38.3 transport test report, UK Batteries & Accumulators Regulations 2008 registration, UK EPR producer registration, and appointment of a UK Responsible Person are the major UK compliance…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/lithium-battery-china-to-uk
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/lithium-battery-china-to-uk.json
- Lithium battery / power bank -> United States (UL / DOT / UN38.3): To export lithium batteries or power banks from China to the United States, you need: (1) NRTL safety listing under UL 1642 (cells), UL 2054 (packs), or UL 2056 (power banks) — there is no single federal mandatory mark, but NRTL listing…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/lithium-battery-china-to-us
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/lithium-battery-china-to-us.json
- Lithium battery / power bank -> Vietnam (TCVN / QCVN): Chinese lithium battery and power bank exporters to Vietnam must meet TCVN/QCVN conformity assessment with the CR mark (chung nhan hop quy) where a QCVN applies, typically on a TCVN IEC 62133 basis, obtain MIC type approval for any…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/lithium-battery-china-to-vietnam
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/lithium-battery-china-to-vietnam.json

- PPE / respirator (mask) -> Australia (AS/NZS 1716, WHS, TGA medical devices): For Australian workplace respiratory protection, respirators are expected to be certified to AS/NZS 1716 and used under an appropriate respiratory protection program, with selection, fit, training, maintenance, and risk controls driven…

  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/ppe-respirator-china-to-uk

- PPE / respirator (mask) -> United States (NIOSH / FDA): Chinese KN95 certification (GB 2626) is NOT equivalent to US NIOSH N95 approval (42 CFR Part 84) — there is no mutual recognition, and COVID-era EUAs permitting KN95 imports have expired. All particulate respirators sold or used in US…

## Wireless / IoT device
- Wireless / IoT device -> Australia (ACMA / EESS / RCM): The main Australia gaps for a China-made Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, or IoT device are ACMA radiocommunications compliance under the Radiocommunications Equipment (General) Rules 2021, ACMA EMC compliance, telecommunications customer equipment…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/wireless-device-china-to-australia
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/wireless-device-china-to-australia.json
- Wireless / IoT device -> Canada (ISED radio, ICES, safety, bilingual labelling): For a China-made wireless or IoT device entering Canada, the hard gaps are ISED certification to applicable RSS standards such as RSS-247, display of the ISED certification number commonly called the IC number, ICES compliance for…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/wireless-device-china-to-canada
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/wireless-device-china-to-canada.json
- Wireless / IoT device -> European Union (RED 2014/53): Radio performance (EN 300 328 / EN 301 893), EMC (EN 301 489), electrical safety (EN IEC 62368-1:2020+A11), and cybersecurity under RED Art. 3.3(d)-(f) mandatory from 1 August 2025 (EN 18031) are the major EU gaps compared with Chinese…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/wireless-device-china-to-eu
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/wireless-device-china-to-eu.json
- Wireless / IoT device -> India: The main India gaps for a China-origin wireless/IoT device are WPC Equipment Type Approval (ETA) for de-licensed radio modules, WPC/SACFA clearance where licensed spectrum or fixed radio sites are involved, BIS CRS registration for…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/wireless-device-china-to-india
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/wireless-device-china-to-india.json
- Wireless / IoT device -> United Kingdom (UKCA / Radio Eqpt Regs): Radio Equipment Regulations 2017 (UKCA mark, BS EN 300 328 / 301 893), the PSTI Act 2022 IoT security baselines (unique passwords, vulnerability disclosure, update period — in force April 2024), and appointment of a UK Responsible Person…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/wireless-device-china-to-uk
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/wireless-device-china-to-uk.json
- Wireless / IoT device -> United States (FCC): FCC Certification via a TCB (Part 15 Subpart C/E for Wi-Fi/Bluetooth), a Supplier's Declaration of Conformity for unintentional radiators (Part 15 Subpart B), FCC ID labelling, designation of a US agent, and NRTL listing to UL 62368-1…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/wireless-device-china-to-us
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/wireless-device-china-to-us.json

## Textiles & apparel
- Textiles & apparel -> Australia (ACCC / Product Safety Australia): Australia does not require CE marking for textiles and apparel. The main China-to-Australia gaps are: mandatory permanent care instructions aligned to AS/NZS 1957; children's nightwear fire-hazard category and warning labelling under the…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/textiles-apparel-china-to-australia
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/textiles-apparel-china-to-australia.json
- Textiles & apparel -> Canada (Textile Labelling / CCPSA): Canada-bound textiles and apparel are not CE-marked and do not use an EU-style harmonised-standard route. The main gaps for Chinese exporters are: (1) mandatory fibre-content and dealer-identity labelling under the Textile Labelling Act…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/textiles-apparel-china-to-canada
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/textiles-apparel-china-to-canada.json
- Textiles & apparel -> European Union (REACH / Textile Labelling): EU textiles are NOT CE-marked. The four main compliance gaps for Chinese textile and apparel exporters entering the EU are: (1) REACH Annex XVII chemical restrictions — particularly NPE (nonylphenol ethoxylates) and CMR substances not…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/textiles-apparel-china-to-eu
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/textiles-apparel-china-to-eu.json
- Textiles & apparel -> United Kingdom (UK REACH / Labelling): The principal compliance gaps for Chinese textiles and apparel entering the UK are: UK REACH Annex XVII azo dye testing to BS EN ISO 14362, mandatory English-language fibre composition labels (SI 2012/1102) with the UK importer's name,…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/textiles-apparel-china-to-uk
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/textiles-apparel-china-to-uk.json
- Textiles & apparel -> United States (FTC / CPSC): FTC fibre-content and care labelling (English, permanent), CPSC flammability testing (16 CFR 1610), mandatory third-party CPC and tracking labels for children's products, and UFLPA forced-labor supply-chain documentation for cotton goods…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/textiles-apparel-china-to-us
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/textiles-apparel-china-to-us.json

## Furniture
- Furniture -> Australia: For Chinese furniture exported to Australia, the main gaps are: mandatory ACCC toppling furniture warning and safety-information rules from 4 May 2025 for covered clothing storage units, bookcases, entertainment units, hall tables,…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/furniture-china-to-australia
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/furniture-china-to-australia.json
- Furniture -> Canada (CCPSA / Health Canada): For Chinese furniture exported to Canada, the main gaps are CCPSA general product safety and incident-documentation duties, mandatory mattress cigarette-ignition flammability under SOR/2016-183, composite wood formaldehyde limits and…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/furniture-china-to-canada
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/furniture-china-to-canada.json
- Furniture -> European Union (REACH / GPSR): REACH formaldehyde limits (stricter than Chinese E1), GPSR general safety documentation and EU Responsible Person, SVHC/azo dye restricted substance screening, EUDR deforestation due diligence for wood products, and the…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/furniture-china-to-eu
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/furniture-china-to-eu.json
- Furniture -> United Kingdom (Fire Safety Regs): The Furniture and Furnishings (Fire Safety) Regulations 1988 are the biggest UK-specific gap — they require mandatory cigarette and match resistance testing to BS 5852 plus permanent labelling on all upholstered furniture, with no EU…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/furniture-china-to-uk
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/furniture-china-to-uk.json
- Furniture -> United States (CARB/TSCA / CPSC): For Chinese furniture exported to the US, the four highest-priority compliance items are: (1) EPA TSCA Title VI third-party certification for composite wood panels (mandatory federal law); (2) STURDY Act 16 CFR Part 1261 tip-over…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/furniture-china-to-us
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/furniture-china-to-us.json

## Power tool
- Power tool -> Australia (RCM): For China-made corded or cordless power tools entering Australia, the mandatory baseline is Australian electrical safety law as applied through EESS/RCM for in-scope equipment, ACMA EMC compliance for regulated electrical products, and…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/power-tool-china-to-australia
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/power-tool-china-to-australia.json
- Power tool -> Brazil (INMETRO): For China-made electric or cordless power tools entering Brazil, the mandatory baseline is INMETRO compulsory conformity certification under the applicable Portaria using ABNT NBR IEC 62841 safety testing through an accredited OCP, EMC…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/power-tool-china-to-brazil
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/power-tool-china-to-brazil.json
- Power tool -> Canada: For China-made corded or cordless power tools entering Canada, the practical baseline is Canadian electrical equipment approval through an SCC-accredited certification body, commonly using the CSA C22.2/UL 62841 binational power-tool…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/power-tool-china-to-canada
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/power-tool-china-to-canada.json
- Power tool -> Chile (SEC): For China-made electric or cordless power tools entering Chile, the mandatory baseline is SEC electrical product certification through an authorized certifier against the applicable NCh IEC standards (commonly the NCh IEC 62841 series);…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/power-tool-china-to-chile
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/power-tool-china-to-chile.json
- Power tool -> Egypt (EOS / GOEIC): For China-made electric or cordless power tools entering Egypt, the mandatory baseline is conformity with applicable EOS (Egyptian) standards (many of which adopt IEC 62841), GOEIC importer and foreign-factory registration under the…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/power-tool-china-to-egypt
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/power-tool-china-to-egypt.json
- Power tool -> European Union (CE): For China-made electric or cordless power tools entering the EU, the mandatory baseline is CE compliance under machinery law, LVD where applicable, EMC, outdoor noise for listed outdoor equipment, RoHS, and the EU Battery Regulation for…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/power-tool-china-to-eu
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/power-tool-china-to-eu.json
- Power tool -> India (BIS): For China-made corded or cordless power tools entering India, the mandatory baseline is BIS certification or CRS registration where the tool, charger, battery, or cell is covered by a notified QCO or CRS category; in-scope IS 302 or…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/power-tool-china-to-india
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/power-tool-china-to-india.json
- Power tool -> Indonesia (SNI / Kemenperin): For power tools from China to Indonesia, the high-friction points are Indonesia’s mandatory SNI pathway based on IEC 62841 adoption, LSPro/SPPT-SNI mark readiness, and in-country importer responsibilities, while cordless products with…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/power-tool-china-to-indonesia
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/power-tool-china-to-indonesia.json
- Power tool -> Japan (PSE/JET): For China-made electric or cordless power tools entering Japan, the mandatory baseline is DENAN (Electrical Appliances and Materials Safety Act) PSE conformity for in-scope corded tools and AC chargers/adaptors, with a Japan-resident…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/power-tool-china-to-japan
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/power-tool-china-to-japan.json
- Power tool -> Kenya (KEBS): For China-made power tools entering Kenya, the mandatory baseline is KEBS PVoC import conformity for regulated models, a Certificate of Conformity, and the Standardization/Import Standardization Mark at clear-in process. Power-tool body…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/power-tool-china-to-kenya
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/power-tool-china-to-kenya.json
- Power tool -> Malaysia (ST / SIRIM): For power tools, Malaysia entry is commonly driven by ST-coordinated CoA control through SIRIM QAS with Malaysia-resident importer responsibility, 240 V and 50 Hz suitability evidence, and MCMC handling for wireless or cordless functions…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/power-tool-china-to-malaysia
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/power-tool-china-to-malaysia.json
- Power tool -> Mexico (NOM): For China-made electric or cordless power tools entering Mexico, the mandatory baseline is NOM electrical safety certification under NOM-003-SCFI with the NOM mark issued through an accredited body such as NYCE or ANCE, an in-country…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/power-tool-china-to-mexico
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/power-tool-china-to-mexico.json
- Power tool -> Morocco (IMANOR): For China-made power tools entering Morocco, the mandatory entry baseline is IMANOR NM-aligned electrical safety and EMC evidence, Ministry of Industry import-conformity attestation with a local importer, ANRT type approval for…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/power-tool-china-to-morocco
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/power-tool-china-to-morocco.json
- Power tool -> Nigeria (SON / SONCAP): For China-made electric or cordless power tools entering Nigeria, the mandatory baseline is SONCAP under the Standards Organisation of Nigeria: a Product Certificate plus a per-shipment SONCAP Certificate are required before customs…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/power-tool-china-to-nigeria
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/power-tool-china-to-nigeria.json
- Power tool -> Philippines (BPS / DTI): For power tools exported from China to the Philippines, do not assume Chinese GB/T evidence or a CCC mark carries over. The defensible starting point is to confirm whether the specific tool falls under a Philippine mandatory product…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/power-tool-china-to-philippines
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/power-tool-china-to-philippines.json
- Power tool -> Saudi Arabia (SASO/SABER): For China-made power tools entering Saudi Arabia, the key practical gap is that Chinese CCC or GB/T evidence is not enough by itself. Saudi importers in-country usually need SABER registration, model-level conformity work for SASO…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/power-tool-china-to-saudi
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/power-tool-china-to-saudi.json
- Power tool -> Singapore (EMA / Enterprise Singapore): For China-made power tools entering Singapore, the key access question is whether the item is a Controlled Good under the Consumer Protection (Safety Requirements) Registration Scheme administered by Enterprise Singapore; household-type…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/power-tool-china-to-singapore
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/power-tool-china-to-singapore.json
- Power tool -> South Africa (NRCS): For China-made electric or cordless power tools entering South Africa, the baseline is electrical safety to SANS IEC 62841 (South Africa adopts the IEC series via SABS), an NRCS Letter of Authority where the tool falls under a Compulsory…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/power-tool-china-to-south-africa
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/power-tool-china-to-south-africa.json
- Power tool -> South Korea (KC / KATS): For China-made electric or cordless power tools entering South Korea, the mandatory baseline is KC Safety Certification under the Electrical Appliances and Consumer Products Safety Control Act, KS C IEC 62841 electrical safety, KC-EMC…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/power-tool-china-to-south-korea
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/power-tool-china-to-south-korea.json
- Power tool -> Thailand (TISI): For power tools from China entering Thailand, the practical entry gates are TISI applicability, TIS marking for controlled products, NBTC telecom checks for cordless radio functions, and in-country importer/route management via Thailand…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/power-tool-china-to-thailand
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/power-tool-china-to-thailand.json
- Power tool -> Turkey (TSE / CE): For Turkey-facing power tools, the practical baseline is CE-based machinery conformity with EN 62841 as the common technical standard family, EN 62841 and EU-reference harmonisation mapped through TSE TS EN where available, LVD and EMC…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/power-tool-china-to-turkey
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/power-tool-china-to-turkey.json
- Power tool -> United Arab Emirates (MoIAT/ESMA — ECAS): Chinese exporters should not assume a China compliance packet is accepted as-is in UAE. Start from MoIAT (ESMA legacy) product scope, secure ECAS/EQM route for applicable categories, align voltage/frequency assumptions to UAE 230/400 V…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/power-tool-china-to-uae
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/power-tool-china-to-uae.json
- Power tool -> United Kingdom (UKCA or CE accepted in Great Britain): For China-made electric or cordless power tools entering Great Britain, the mandatory baseline is compliance under the Supply of Machinery (Safety) Regulations 2008, Electrical Equipment (Safety) Regulations 2016 where applicable, EMC…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/power-tool-china-to-uk
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/power-tool-china-to-uk.json
- Power tool -> United States: The US has no single federal power-tool mark. For practical market access, Chinese power-tool exporters usually need NRTL listing to UL 62841 or legacy UL 60745 for electric shock and fire safety, FCC Part 15 authorization if electronics…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/power-tool-china-to-us
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/power-tool-china-to-us.json
- Power tool -> Vietnam (TCVN / QCVN): For China-made electric or cordless power tools entering Vietnam, the practical baseline is product safety to TCVN IEC 62841 (Vietnam adoption of the IEC 62841 series), conformity to any applicable QCVN national technical regulation…
  page: https://asaptic.com/standard/power-tool-china-to-vietnam
  data: https://asaptic.com/standard/exports/power-tool-china-to-vietnam.json
